sections.title: A city planner talks about smart services

content.audioNote: Teacher plays audio when available, or reads the transcript aloud.
content.transcript:
Host: Welcome to City Voices. Today we speak with Dana, an urban planner in Riyadh.
Dana: Thank you. I usually visit neighbourhoods on Sundays, and this month I am reviewing how smart bins and street sensors help cleaning teams. Citizens report problems in an app, and we respond faster. The goal is not technology for its own sake — it is a cleaner, safer city for everyone.
prediction: What smart services might a city planner mention?

whileListening.prompt: When does Dana usually visit neighbourhoods?
What is she reviewing this month?

pairWork.title: Resident + planner
steps: Student A is a resident. Describe one local problem (traffic, litter, heat, parking).
Student B is a planner. Suggest one smart or simple solution and one question.

groupDiscussion.title: What makes a city ‘smart’?
groupDiscussion.prompt: In groups of 3–4, agree on the top THREE features of a smart city for Saudi students your age. Be ready to report to the class.

rolePlay.title: Community meeting
roles: Chairperson: Welcome people and keep time.
Planner: Offer a respectful solution and invite ideas.
successCriteria: One Present Simple fact + one Present Continuous temporary action
